course_title: The Romantic Movement in U.S. Literature 1810–1860
description:
Offered: every fourth semester
This course begins by considering similarities between British and American Romanticism, then it explores the movement’s orientations towards nature, the supernatural, race, gender, and the individual’s role in society. Key authors for this course include Emerson, Thoreau, Poe, Melville, Dickinson, and Whitman. While the focus will be on careful readings of selected texts, we occasionally consider how principles of Romanticism appear in the arts of painting, music, and dance.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200 or HON 1200
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, IHIP, LI
